context: A psychometric instrument assessing Gen Z attitudes toward social media's role in political engagement and activism in 2025. This survey captures how young adults (ages 18-26) perceive social media as a tool for political expression, their trust in information sources, and their views on the relationship between online activism and real-world political change. The instrument explores both optimistic and cynical perspectives on digital political participation.

questions.preprompt: Thinking about {player_name}'s ability to create political change, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} believes that posting about political issues on social media can actually influence government policy.

questions.preprompt: Thinking about {player_name}'s ability to create political change, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} feels that their political voice matters more when expressed online than in traditional voting or community organizing.

questions.preprompt: Thinking about {player_name}'s ability to create political change, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} thinks that most online political activism is performative and doesn't lead to meaningful real-world change.

questions.preprompt: Considering {player_name}'s approach to consuming political information, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} trusts the political information they encounter on TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube more than traditional news outlets.

questions.preprompt: Considering {player_name}'s approach to consuming political information, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} is confident they can distinguish between reliable political information and misinformation on social media.

questions.preprompt: Considering {player_name}'s approach to consuming political information, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} is concerned that social media algorithms are feeding them a distorted view of political reality.

questions.preprompt: Reflecting on {player_name}'s own online political engagement, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} feels genuine passion when they post about political causes on social media.

questions.preprompt: Reflecting on {player_name}'s own online political engagement, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} sometimes posts political content primarily because they want to appear engaged or socially conscious to their followers.

questions.preprompt: Reflecting on {player_name}'s own online political engagement, to what extent would {player_name} agree with the following statement:
questions.statement: {player_name} believes that their online political activism reflects their actual values and commitments.
